meta.tagline: Multi-location SoCal mover · #1 on Maps in Santa Clarita
meta.author: Aleksandar Spasevski
meta.doc_subtitle: Defend #1 Santa Clarita Maps · accelerate Q2 2026 organic recovery · strengthen multi-location page network · capitalize on packing/storage momentum.

tldr: AQMS is the #1-ranked Santa Clarita mover on Google Maps (avg position 3.17 across 727 grid cells, 557 top-3 placements — more than the next two competitors combined) and operates a multi-location SoCal page network (Santa Clarita + San Diego + Burbank + Encinitas + La Jolla + Temecula). The site has 21,375 ranked keywords with 5,732 already in top-10. HQDM engaged in January 2026 — month one coincided with a sharp organic cliff (sessions Dec 400 → Jan 97 → Feb 75 = −81% in 2 months) driven by an algorithm update layered on top of a prior vendor's traffic-tool unwinding. Recovery work began immediately: organic sessions Feb 75 → Mar 109 → Apr 320 → May 337 (4.5× the trough). Q1 vs Q2 2026: organic sessions +134%. Maps position is up too: +46 top-3 placements since engagement start (Mar 8 → May 3), with every one of the six priority queries gaining ground. Form-submission tracking broke Apr 14 (CF7 update silently disabled the post-submit redirect that the GA4 trigger was wired to); 70 form submissions recovered from WordPress Flamingo for the gap window — combined ~40 leads in April and ~47 in May (first 28 days). The form itself never broke; the GA4 backfill is in flight via the dev team.

headline_panel.headline: Month-one cliff → 4-month recovery. Organic sessions up 4.5× from the Feb trough; last 90 days +34%.
headline_panel.lede: HQDM engaged in January 2026 — the same month an algorithm update + a prior vendor's traffic-tool unwinding produced an −81% organic cliff (Dec 400 → Feb 75 sessions). Recovery work began immediately; by May, organic sessions are at 337 (4.5× the Feb low) and the last 90 days are up +34% in organic visitors versus the prior 90. The Maps position never broke — and in fact extended its lead, +46 top-3 placements since engagement start. The next 90 days are about compounding the recovery. Lead-conversion metrics are excluded this quarter while website form-submission tracking is being stabilized — see chart caption for detail.

callout_strip.label: What HQDM inherited
callout_strip.detail: Jan 2026 algorithm pressure layered on a prior vendor's Direct-traffic-tool unwinding (Oct-Dec 2025: 1,103 → 1,901 → 1,288 sess crashed to 54 in Jan — classic ATB-bot signature). Organic also crashed 400 → 75 across Jan-Feb.

callout_strip.label: What we've fixed
callout_strip.detail: Stopped the bot/automation source · halted any active link-tool campaigns · refocused work onto the channels that survived (Maps + GBP). Organic recovery curve confirms the cleanup is working.

callout_strip.label: Why Maps is the lead
callout_strip.detail: Maps position never broke through the cliff (#1 in market). GBP+Referral session volume is up YoY and Maps top-3 placements are growing. The next 90 days compound the channel that proved resilient + accelerate organic before competitors close.

trend.headline: 18-month organic + multi-channel — Q1 2026 cliff + Q2 2026 recovery curve
trend.annotation_text: Organic Search trough Jan-Feb 2026 (75-97 sessions) recovering rapidly post-engagement (Mar → May = 109 → 320 → 337). Direct spike Oct-Dec 2025 (1,103-1,901/mo) was likely ATB-bot artifact from prior vendor stack — crashed to 54 in Jan 2026 alongside organic. Paid Search died around Apr 2025 (separate ads-account issue).
trend.may_extrapolation_note: May 2026 = first 22 days actual GA4 (partial month). Full-month projection ~+30%.

organic_split.note: Splits the GA4 "Organic Search" channel into (a) true Google Search arrivals and (b) GBP-tagged sessions arriving via the GBP "Click to website" button (utm_source=gmb). Site uses utm_medium=organic so GA4 buckets these into Organic Search. Lead counts use form_submit pre-2026 and contact_form_submit 2026 onward, with the Apr 14 → May 28 gap recovered from Oleg's Flamingo backfill.

events.label: Pre-HQDM: Direct spike begins (Oct 2025) — ATB-bot signature
HQDM engagement starts (Jan 2026); cliff onset same month

yoy_growth.headline: YoY: Q2 2026* (partial, in-progress) vs Q2 2025 (baseline)
yoy_growth.current_definition: April 2026 + May 2026 partial (first 22 days) — partial-quarter view, NOT a full-Q projection. Form-submission counts include the 70-event backfill from WordPress Flamingo (Apr 14 → May 28) attributed mostly to Direct channel where they will land in GA4 via Measurement Protocol. Pre-2026 = form_submit eventCount; 2026 onward = contact_form_submit (the canonical event after CF7 migration).
yoy_growth.reading: Across all channels, sessions are nearly flat YoY (-7%) and form submissions are slightly UP (+3%) — the cliff is fully behind us on the lead side. Organic sessions are still recovering (-47% on the YoY average since the cliff sits in the comparison window), but GBP+Referral is +55% YoY and the last-90-day organic recovery is +34%. Form-submission rate is up +11% — recovering visitors are converting better than last year. Q2 2026 partial = 39 + 47 = 86 form submissions across all channels (~130/qtr full-quarter pace, vs Q2 25 = 125).

llm_visibility.headline: AI Overview saturation on AQMS keywords: 20% (4/20). AQMS cited in zero AIOs. Local Pack still dominates commercial queries (14/20).
llm_visibility.lede: Live DataForSEO SERP pull (2026-05-22) on 20 priority AQMS keywords spanning commercial geo / commercial service / informational / mid-funnel. Commercial-geo + commercial-service queries don't trigger AIO yet — all 10 still show Local Pack as the dominant feature. Informational + mid-funnel queries are the AIO migration front: 4 of 10 informational/mid-funnel queries now have AIOs, and AQMS is not cited in any of them. Mid-funnel cost queries (moving cost santa clarita already has an AIO) are the timing-correct target for content + citation work.

implications: AIO is NOT yet at commercial-geo / commercial-service queries. 10/10 commercial queries surface Local Pack. Lever A (GBP defense) is the right lever for these.

Mid-funnel + informational queries are the AIO migration front. 4 priority queries already have AIOs; AQMS not cited in any. Lever E targets these with cost + how-to content optimized for AIO citation.
AQMS has 8 queries already in top-10 organic. Defense + minor depth upgrades on these compound the existing position.

levers.name: Defend the #1 Santa Clarita Maps position
levers.why: AQMS is #1 in market and extended the lead in 2 months (+46 top-3 placements Mar 8 → May 3, avg rank 3.37 → 3.17). Maps is the channel that survived the Q1 cliff intact + GBP+Referral leads up 167% YoY — every defense lever compounds the highest-converting channel. J&G's +124 gain is the one to watch.

direct_referral_analysis.headline: What harmed: a fake-Direct spike Oct–Dec 2025. What works: Craigslist (54 conv, free). What's untapped: Nextdoor, Apartments.com, mover marketplaces.

what_harmed.title: The Q4 2025 Direct-traffic spike was fake
what_harmed.detail: Direct sessions jumped Oct 1,103 → Nov 1,901 → Dec 1,288 → crashed to 54 in Jan. Average engagement rate on those sessions was abnormally low. This is the classic signature of an automated 'traffic tool' or a 3rd-party referral spamming Direct attribution. The traffic was inflating session counts but not contributing real leads — when it stopped, the algorithm appears to have rebalanced rankings shortly after, layering the organic cliff on top.
evidence: Direct sessions on /careers/ landing 330/18mo with 30% CVR — but those are job applicants (form_submit on careers), not customer leads. Real customer Direct CVR is closer to 3%.
Direct sessions on (not set) landing page: 195 / 2 conv / 2.6% engagement = unmistakable bot footprint.
Real Direct (homepage, /contact/, /santa-clarita/, location pages) is now ~600-680 sess/mo (Apr-May 2026) and converting normally — that's brand recall + bookmarks, not bots.

what_works.title: Channels already driving real leads (free or near-free)

rows.note: ★ Star finding — Craigslist Services 'Skilled Trades' or 'Labor/Move' listings convert at 28%. Likely a single listing or a few. Worth scaling.
LLM referrals (people clicking ChatGPT citations). Emerging channel — see also Perplexity (10/0).
The Master's University alumni site. Local-institution channel — replicable for Santa Clarita schools/employers.
Email signatures + replies linking to AQMS. Highest CVR in the set — passive lead source.
Shared Google Docs — partnership / contract / quote-doc click-throughs.
San Diego Chamber of Commerce. Confirms multi-location reach; replicate in Santa Clarita / Burbank chambers.
Local Santa Clarita Valley jobs board. Mostly HR traffic (likely /careers/ landings).

junk_sources.note: Lead-gen aggregator. Zero conversions — likely scraper or low-quality referral. Worth blocking in GA4 + checking if a paid agreement is feeding this.

untapped_ideas.why: Already 28% CVR on 192 sess. Likely a single SCV listing. Adding listings for Burbank + San Diego service areas, plus weekly bumps, could 3-5× the volume.
Free 'Local Deals' + paid 'Sponsorship' formats. Hyper-local — perfect fit for a multi-location SoCal mover. Highest-CVR demographic (homeowners moving).
Apartments.com 'Moving Center' lets local movers list. Zillow mover-deals widget on listing pages. Property managers + realtors drive ~30% of moving-decision discovery.
Direct referral fee or co-branded discount card. The Craigslist + alumni.masters.edu signals show local-trust referrals work — formalize the strongest 3-5.

untapped_ideas.channel: Chambers of Commerce (Santa Clarita, Burbank, San Diego)
untapped_ideas.why: San Diego chamber referral already converting. Replicate the listing in all 3 metros where AQMS has presence.
Pay-per-job marketplaces with screened buyer intent. Profile + rating system rewards AQMS's 452-review reputation. Test with 5–10 jobs first.
37 ChatGPT + 10 Perplexity sessions already. Build LLM-citable content (cost calculators, comparison guides) — see Lever E in the Q3 strategy.
Search-driven Reddit traffic is sustained; community-engaged accounts get organic referrals. Low-risk soft-build channel.
